name: ITU Constitution 1992 (International Telecommunication Union)
summary: The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-ITU-treaty framework adopted by the ITU member states in Geneva — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-telecommunication-and-ICT framework + oldest UN specialized agency framework + only UN agency operating formal sector-membership framework via private-sector members (ITU Constitution and Convention adopted 22 December 1992 in Geneva + effective 1 July 1994 + International Telegraph Union founded 17 May 1865 in Paris by 20 founding states + renamed International Telecommunication Union 1934 + UN specialized agency from 15 October 1947 framework — formal foundational ITU treaty framework establishing comprehensive ITU organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-telecommunication-and-ICT framework + oldest UN specialized agency framework + only UN agency operating formal sector-membership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three Sectors framework: ITU-R Radiocommunication + ITU-T Standardization + ITU-D Development framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Plenipotentiary Conference framework via quadrennial-PP conferences framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Radio Regulations RR framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal International Mobile Telecommunications IMT-2020-5G + IMT-2030-6G framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Resolution 70 Gender-Mainstreaming framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal World Telecommunication and Information Society Day 17 May framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WSIS World Summit on the Information Society 2003 + 2005 + WSIS+20 2025 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal AI for Good 2017 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Global Digital Compact 2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Chinese-Doreen-Bogdan-Martin-American-PP-2022 framework via Chinese ITU Secretary-General candidate vs American Doreen Bogdan-Martin US-China-technology-competition framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Indigenous-Languages-Decade 2022-2032 ITU framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal ITU Three Sectors framework (ITU Three Sectors providing formal sectoral framework: ITU-R Radiocommunication + ITU-T Standardization + ITU-D Development framework — formal foundational ITU Three Sectors framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three Sectors framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal ITU Radio Regulations RR framework (ITU Radio Regulations RR providing formal international-radio-frequency allocations framework + updated by World Radiocommunication Conferences WRC quadrennially framework — formal foundational ITU Radio Regulations RR framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-radio-frequency allocations framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WRC quadrennial framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good framework (World Summit on the Information Society WSIS held 10-12 December 2003 in Geneva + 16-18 November 2005 in Tunis + WSIS+20 commemorated 2025 framework + Global Digital Compact adopted 22 September 2024 + AI for Good Summit launched 2017 framework — formal foundational WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good framework). The ITU Constitution is binding upon 194 ITU member states + 900+ ITU sector members from private-sector framework — substantial universal coverage framework with notable non-members North Korea + 2-3 micro-states. The Constitution is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal ITU Child Online Protection COP framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal cross-border-online-grooming-children framework + ITU Resolution 179 framework + ITU Online-Safety-for-Children-of-Separated-Parents framework. The ITU Plenipotentiary Conference PP + ITU Council + ITU Secretary-General + ITU Three Sector Directors + ITU Radio Regulations Board RRB are the formal ITU bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The ITU Constitution is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: ITU Constitution and Convention
statutory_framework.relevance: ITU Constitution and Convention adopted 22 December 1992 in Geneva + effective 1 July 1994 — formal foundational ITU treaty framework establishing comprehensive ITU organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: ITU Radio Regulations
statutory_framework.relevance: ITU Radio Regulations RR adopted 1906 + updated quadrennially by World Radiocommunication Conferences WRC — formal foundational ITU Radio Regulations framework.

statutory_framework.title: WSIS
statutory_framework.relevance: World Summit on the Information Society WSIS held 10-12 December 2003 in Geneva + 16-18 November 2005 in Tunis — formal foundational WSIS framework.

statutory_framework.title: ITU Child Online Protection COP
statutory_framework.relevance: ITU Child Online Protection COP launched 2008 — formal foundational ITU Child Online Protection framework with PA-intersection framework.

statutory_framework.title: Global Digital Compact
statutory_framework.relevance: Global Digital Compact adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York — formal foundational Global Digital Compact framework.

apex_courts.name: ITU Plenipotentiary Conference (PP)
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for ITU.

apex_courts.name: ITU Council
apex_courts.role: Formal executive body for ITU composed of 48 member states.

apex_courts.name: ITU Secretary-General
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for ITU — ITU Secretary-General Doreen Bogdan-Martin 2023-2026+ first female ITU Secretary-General framework.

apex_courts.name: ITU Three Sector Directors
apex_courts.role: Formal ITU Three Sector Directors body: ITU-R Radiocommunication + ITU-T Standardization + ITU-D Development.

apex_courts.name: ITU Radio Regulations Board (RRB)
apex_courts.role: Formal ITU Radio Regulations Board body composed of 12 elected members.

anonymisation_convention: ITU-related communications operate via state-state framework via ITU PP + Council; individual communications limited under ITU framework + private-sector engagement via ITU Sector Members framework.

key_developments.title: International Telegraph Union founded
key_developments.description: International Telegraph Union founded 17 May 1865 in Paris by 20 founding states + first international treaty organization framework — predecessor framework to ITU.

key_developments.title: ITU Radio Regulations first adopted
key_developments.description: ITU Radio Regulations RR first adopted 1906 at International Radiotelegraph Convention Berlin — formal foundational ITU Radio Regulations framework adopted.

key_developments.title: ITU renamed International Telecommunication Union
key_developments.description: International Telegraph Union renamed International Telecommunication Union 1934 by Madrid Convention — formal ITU renaming framework operational.

key_developments.title: ITU becomes UN specialized agency
key_developments.description: ITU became UN specialized agency 15 October 1947 — formal ITU UN specialized agency framework operational.

key_developments.title: ITU Constitution and Convention
key_developments.description: ITU Constitution and Convention adopted 22 December 1992 in Geneva at additional Plenipotentiary Conference — formal foundational ITU Constitution and Convention framework adopted.

key_developments.title: ITU Constitution enters force + Three Sectors operational
key_developments.description: ITU Constitution and Convention entered force 1 July 1994 + Three Sectors operational ITU-R + ITU-T + ITU-D framework — formal foundational ITU Constitution + Three Sectors framework operational.

key_developments.title: WSIS Geneva
key_developments.description: World Summit on the Information Society WSIS held 10-12 December 2003 in Geneva — formal foundational WSIS Geneva framework operational.

key_developments.title: WSIS Tunis + Internet Governance Forum
key_developments.description: WSIS held 16-18 November 2005 in Tunis + Tunis Agenda + Internet Governance Forum IGF established 2006 — formal foundational WSIS Tunis + IGF framework operational.

key_developments.title: ITU Child Online Protection COP launched
key_developments.description: ITU Child Online Protection COP launched 2008 — formal foundational ITU Child Online Protection framework operational.

key_developments.title: AI for Good Summit launched
key_developments.description: AI for Good Summit launched 2017 — formal foundational AI for Good framework operational.

key_developments.title: Doreen Bogdan-Martin elected ITU Secretary-General first female + Chinese-American PP
key_developments.description: Doreen Bogdan-Martin US elected ITU Secretary-General 29 September 2022 at PP-22 Bucharest Romania first female ITU Secretary-General + defeating Russian candidate Rashid Ismailov + Chinese ITU Secretary-General candidate framework — formal foundational Doreen Bogdan-Martin + US-China-technology-competition framework operational.

key_developments.title: Global Digital Compact + Pact for the Future
key_developments.description: Global Digital Compact adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York + Pact for the Future framework — formal foundational Global Digital Compact framework operational.

key_developments.title: WSIS+20 commemoration + WRC-23 results + IMT-2030-6G + UN80
key_developments.description: WSIS+20 commemoration 2025 + WRC-23 results 20 November - 15 December 2023 in Dubai UAE + IMT-2030-6G framework + UN80 Reform Process 2025-2026 framework — formal WSIS+20 + WRC-23 + IMT-2030-6G + UN80 framework operational.

structural_findings: ITU Constitution operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places ITU Constitution in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-telecommunication-and-ICT framework + oldest UN specialized agency framework + only UN agency operating formal sector-membership framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-telecommunication-and-ICT framework + oldest UN specialized agency framework via 17 May 1865 founding + only UN agency operating formal sector-membership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three Sectors framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Plenipotentiary Conference framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Radio Regulations RR framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal International Mobile Telecommunications IMT-2020-5G + IMT-2030-6G framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Resolution 70 Gender-Mainstreaming framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal World Telecommunication and Information Society Day 17 May framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WSIS World Summit on the Information Society 2003 + 2005 + WSIS+20 2025 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal AI for Good 2017 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Global Digital Compact 2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Chinese-Doreen-Bogdan-Martin-American-PP-2022 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Indigenous-Languages-Decade 2022-2032 ITU framework.
Central jurisdiction of formal ITU Three Sectors framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three Sectors framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal ITU Radio Regulations RR framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-radio-frequency allocations framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WRC quadrennial framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good framework.
194 ITU member state + 900+ ITU sector member ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial universal coverage framework.

1865 ITU founded + 1906 Radio Regulations + 1934 renaming + 1947 UN specialized agency + 1992 ITU Constitution + 1994 entry into force + 2003 WSIS Geneva + 2005 WSIS Tunis + 2008 ITU COP + 2017 AI for Good + 2022 Doreen Bogdan-Martin + 2024 Global Digital Compact + 2025 WSIS+20 + IMT-2030-6G + UN80 ITU-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

ITU Constitution + Convention + Three Sectors + Radio Regulations + WSIS + Global Digital Compact + AI for Good + ITU Child Online Protection COP formal octet framework with formal cross-border-online-grooming-children + ITU Resolution 179 + ITU Online-Safety-for-Children-of-Separated-Parents PA-intersection framework.
